steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to explain how Schubart describes C minor — meaning the answer must include both the source (Schubart's work) and the specific mood he assigns to the key.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The key of C minor is one of various keys, which are sets of musical notes that form the foundations of pieces of music.: defines what a musical key is — background theory, not Schubart's description. ✗
  • Choice B — In *Ideas Toward an Aesthetic of Music*, Schubart describes the mood of C minor.: names the source and that he describes a mood, but omits what mood. ✗
  • Choice C — In *Ideas Toward an Aesthetic of Music*, Schubart describes the mood of C minor as expressing "longing.": gives both the source and the specific mood ("longing"). ✓
  • Choice D — The song "Don't Speak" could be described as expressing "longing.": shifts focus to the song example and removes Schubart as the one making the characterization. ✗
That matches Choice C — In *Ideas Toward an Aesthetic of Music*, Schubart describes the mood of C minor as expressing "longing.".
